evals.prompt: complaint-and-event-handler just handed off a task into the SJS CAPA Log titled '[CAPA Pending — capa-coordinator] Pava Toner Lot 26-02 — 4 Skin Reactions complaints in 21 days'. The brief in the comment says: pattern is itching + redness, all consumers reported within 48h of first use, all from DTC channel, no SAE-level events. complaint-and-event-handler recommends scope = single-lot CAPA, suspected vendor: Element (FOA-ROSE component lot R26-008). Walk this through to the action plan stage. Don't actually write to Asana — draft the NCR record, severity call, NCR→CAPA conversion decision, root cause analysis approach, and corrective + preventive action plans as a markdown plan I can approve.
evals.expected_output: A drafted plan that: (1) opens an NCR with all required intake fields per references/ncr-procedure.md §3.2 (NCR-2026-NNN, source=complaint-trend, category=Product, SKU=Pava Toner, batch=Lot 26-02, vendor=Element, suspected severity=Major per §3.3 single-batch complaint cluster); (2) walks the §4.1 review checklist and recommends NCR→CAPA conversion under the Major band 10-day SLA; (3) drafts CAPA-2026-NNN cover block; (4) recommends Fishbone (systemic, multiple contributing factors possible) over 5 Whys with reasoning, and stages the six-category prompt; (5) drafts corrective + preventive action plans tied to a placeholder root cause until the team fills RCA, with each action having owner role + timeline + evidence requirement, and any SOP-impact preventive action tagged for [SOP Revision Pending — quality-manager]; (6) explicitly names the L2 HITL gates: Operator approves intake/conversion/plan, [Pending QA Lead] on root cause sign-off; (7) does not commit any writes; (8) cross-references back to the complaint-and-event-handler source task.

evals.prompt: Got a single-unit vendor receipt failure: HCT shipped 50 units of the Pava Toner cap (component HCT-CAP-007), one of them arrived with a hairline crack visible on inspection. Caught at receiving, the cracked unit was quarantined, the other 49 passed. HCT has no recurrence pattern in the last 12 months. Open this as an NCR — and walk me through whether this needs a CAPA or closes without one. Don't write to Asana, draft the plan.
evals.expected_output: A drafted plan that: (1) opens NCR-2026-NNN with intake fields (source=vendor-receipt, category=Vendor, vendor=HCT, component=HCT-CAP-007, suspected severity=Minor per §3.3 isolated/contained/no-recurrence); (2) walks §4.1 review checklist and explicitly addresses each item (description specific, severity matches §3.3 Minor criteria, no existing open CAPA on HCT-CAP-007, no pattern across recent closed NCRs, containment confirmed via quarantine); (3) recommends Close — No CAPA per §4.2 branch B and §5; (4) drafts the required justification fields per §5: why no CAPA (cite §3.3 Minor band), what containment closed (quarantine of single unit, 49 others passed), trend exposure (cite no HCT recurrence in 12 months), reviewer (Operator role at v5.2); (5) does NOT escalate to CAPA, does NOT invoke RCA tools, does NOT draft action plans; (6) names the L2 gate (Operator approves at v5.2; steady-state would also need QA Lead on a no-escalate justification per the working procedure); (7) flags that closed-no-CAPA NCRs feed the monthly review cycle per §5.

evals.prompt: A pre-v5.4 batch-lifecycle-tracker handoff just landed in SJS CAPA Log: '[CAPA Request — batch-lifecycle-tracker] Soursop Vit C Lot 25-09 — 3-month accelerated stability fail on Vitamin C assay (89% of label claim, spec is ≥95%)'. This is a Critical NCR — stability failure on an active batch already in market at Ulta and DTC. Walk the FULL CAPA lifecycle: NCR open, conversion to CAPA, root cause analysis (recommend the right tool), corrective + preventive actions, verification, effectiveness review, and closeout. Show me what each phase produces, who approves each gate, and what handoffs fire on close. Don't actually write to Asana — draft as a complete walkthrough I can review before we run it for real.
evals.expected_output: A complete six-phase walkthrough that: (1) §5.1 opens NCR with severity=Critical (active in-market batch, regulatory exposure, label claim failure), 5-business-day SLA on conversion; (2) §5.2 review checklist passes, NCR→CAPA conversion approved by Operator, CAPA-2026-NNN assigned, investigation team drafted (Lab, Production, Operator, Department Manager — flagged that v5.4 batch-lifecycle-tracker would also be on the team post-build); (3) RCA recommendation: 5 Whys (single-batch single-event, sequential causation likely) — but notes Fishbone could be warranted if the lab investigation surfaces multiple contributing factors; provides the 5 Whys scaffold prompts per references/rca-tools.md; (4) §5.3 corrective action plan (e.g., contain remaining inventory, retest next-most-recent batch, communicate stability data to Ulta) and preventive action plan (e.g., adjust stability monitoring frequency, SOP revision on stability spec margin) with owners + dates + evidence requirements; preventive action with SOP impact triggers [SOP Revision Pending — quality-manager]; (5) §5.4 implementation tracking surface, overdue ping cadence; (6) §5.5 verification record template + effectiveness review at Critical 90-day window with leading indicators (next-batch retest, in-market stability sample, no recurrence); explicitly addresses what happens if effectiveness fails (reopen at investigation); (7) §5.6 closeout summary, four handoffs that would fire on close (close-the-loop on source batch task per 6a, no SOP revision unless preventive action mandates it per 6b, no vendor scorecard since this is internal batch per 6c, REGULATORY FEED to [Reg Flag Pending — regulatory-manager] per 6d since active in-market batch + label claim failure has reportability implications); (8) every L2 gate explicitly named — Operator vs. [Pending QA Lead] — with the QA Lead gates clustered on root cause sign-off, verification, effectiveness, and close; (9) batch hold/release decision explicitly deferred to batch-lifecycle-tracker per Out of Scope, with the recommendation surfaced but not decided.
